seoTitle: Kensington Park vs Kenton Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kensington Park and Kenton Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kensington Park vs Kenton Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kensington Park and Kenton Valley.
heading: Kensington Park vs Kenton Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kensington Park, South Australia and Kenton Valley, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kenton Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Kensington Park (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kensington Park or Kenton Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kensington Park has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Kenton Valley scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kensington Park scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Kenton Valley scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kensington Park or Kenton Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kenton Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
